title: Bitrix24 Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Bitrix24** is a unified CRM + collaboration suite (CRM leads/contacts/deals/companies, Tasks, Drive, Calendar, Messaging, Lists) exposing a REST API at tenant-scoped portals such as `https://{portal}.bitrix24.com/rest/`. This connector — `Bitrix24Connector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "bitrix24"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Bitrix24 portal:

| Surface | Methods | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| User | `user.current`, `user.get` | Identify the caller + portal users |
| CRM Leads | `crm.lead.list/get/add/update/delete` | Full lead lifecycle |
| CRM Contacts | `crm.contact.list/get/add/update/delete` | Contacts with PHONE/EMAIL multi-value envelopes |
| CRM Companies | `crm.company.list/add` | Companies |
| CRM Deals | `crm.deal.list/get/add/update/delete` | Deal lifecycle |
| CRM Quotes / Invoices | `crm.quote.list`, `crm.invoice.list` | Read quotes + invoices |
| CRM Activities | `crm.activity.list/add` | Audit + log activities |
| Tasks | `tasks.task.list/get/add/update` | Task list, create, update (camelCase keys) |
| Disk | `disk.folder.getchildren` | List Bitrix24 Drive folder children |
| Im (Messaging) | `im.message.add` | Send chat messages |
| Lists | `lists.element.get` | Read Lists-module rows |
| Calendar | `calendar.event.get` | Calendar events |

The connector normalises leads + contacts + deals + tasks into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::Bitrix24HTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate an inbound webhook URL

1. Sign in to your Bitrix24 portal (`https://{portal}.bitrix24.com`).
2. Open **Developer resources → Other → Inbound webhook**.
3. Tick the permissions you need (`CRM`, `Tasks`, `User`, `Im`, `Disk`, `Calendar`, `Lists`).
4. Click **Save** — Bitrix24 generates a URL of the form:

```
https://{portal}.bitrix24.com/rest/{user_id}/{webhook_code}/
```

Copy the URL — it is the credential. Treat it like a password; anyone with the URL can call the granted methods.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Bitrix24**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `webhook_url` (secret, required) | URL from step 4 |
| `access_token` (secret, optional) | Only when using OAuth mode instead of a webhook |
| `portal` (text, optional) | Auto-derived from `webhook_url` |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override the REST base |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 2 (Bitrix24 free tier) |
| `timeout_s` (number) | Per-request timeout, default 30 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the URL is well-formed (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `Bitrix24HTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which posts `user.current.json` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 / `QUERY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Bitrix24 supports two server-to-server auth modes — this connector treats both as `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`.

### Mode A — Inbound webhook URL (default)

The webhook URL itself is the credential. Every method is invoked by POSTing to:

```
{webhook_url}{method}.json
```

No additional headers or query params are required.

### Mode B — OAuth2 access token

Apps that perform the OAuth dance externally end up with an `access_token`. The connector accepts that token + the portal subdomain and calls:

```
POST https://{portal}.bitrix24.com/rest/{method}.json?auth={access_token}
```

Mode A is preferred for tenant-installed integrations; Mode B is for marketplace apps.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `webhook_url` is well-formed OR `access_token` + `portal` are present. Initialises `Bitrix24HTTPClient`. Does **not** call Bitrix24. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo(access_token=credential, token_type="webhook"|"oauth")` — there is no code exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `POST user.current.json` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — credentials are static. |
| token storage | None (webhook URL) or in install_fields (access_token). |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Content-Type: application/json
Accept:       application/json
```

No `Authorization` header. In Mode B, auth travels in the `?auth=` query string.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `webhook_url` | secret | yes (Mode A) | `self.webhook_url` | Inbound webhook URL |
| `access_token` | secret | optional (Mode B) | `self.access_token` | OAuth access token |
| `portal` | text | no | `self.portal` | Auto-derived from `webhook_url` |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` | REST base override |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 2) | Client-side soft cap |
| `timeout_s` | number | no | `self.timeout_s` (default 30) | httpx timeout |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["webhook_url"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

The HTTP base URL is resolved per-request — there is no provider-wide `_BITRIX24_BASE`, every portal has its own host.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `Bitrix24Connector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::Bitrix24HTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `webhook_url` (Mode A) OR `access_token` + `portal`/`base_url` (Mode B); initialise `Bitrix24HTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured credential and `token_type` is `"webhook"` or `"oauth"`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
POSTs `user.current.json`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages CRM leads + contacts + deals + tasks → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: User
children.content:
### `async user_current() -> Dict`
`POST user.current.json`.

### `async list_users(*, start=0) -> Dict`
`POST user.get.json` — Bitrix24 pages by 50.

children.title: CRM — Leads
children.content:
### `async list_leads(*, start=0, select=None, filter=None, order=None) -> Dict`
`POST crm.lead.list.json` — body: `{start, select?, filter?, order?}`.

### `async get_lead(lead_id) -> Dict`
`POST crm.lead.get.json` — body: `{id: lead_id}`.

### `async create_lead(fields) -> Dict`
`POST crm.lead.add.json` — body: `{fields: {...}}`.

### `async update_lead(lead_id, fields) -> Dict`
`POST crm.lead.update.json` — body: `{id, fields}`.

### `async delete_lead(lead_id) -> Dict`
`POST crm.lead.delete.json`.

children.title: CRM — Contacts
children.content:
### `async list_contacts(*, start=0, select=None, filter=None, order=None) -> Dict`
`POST crm.contact.list.json` — defaults `select=['*','PHONE','EMAIL']`.

### `async get_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`POST crm.contact.get.json`.

### `async create_contact(name='', last_name=None, phone=None, email=None, fields=None) -> Dict`
`POST crm.contact.add.json`. Accepts `name`/`last_name`/`phone`/`email` shortcuts — phone/email are normalised to Bitrix24's `[{VALUE, VALUE_TYPE}]` envelopes — or raw `fields`.

### `async update_contact(contact_id, fields) -> Dict`
`POST crm.contact.update.json`.

### `async delete_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`POST crm.contact.delete.json`.

children.title: CRM — Deals + Companies
children.content:
### `async list_companies(*, start=0, select=None, filter=None) -> Dict`
`POST crm.company.list.json`.

### `async create_company(fields) -> Dict`
`POST crm.company.add.json`.

### `async list_deals(*, start=0, select=None, filter=None, order=None) -> Dict`
`POST crm.deal.list.json`.

### `async get_deal(deal_id) -> Dict`
`POST crm.deal.get.json`.

### `async create_deal(fields) -> Dict`
`POST crm.deal.add.json`.

### `async update_deal(deal_id, fields) -> Dict`
`POST crm.deal.update.json`.

### `async delete_deal(deal_id) -> Dict`
`POST crm.deal.delete.json`.

children.title: CRM — Quotes / Invoices / Activities
children.content:
### `async list_quotes(*, start=0) -> Dict`
`POST crm.quote.list.json`.

### `async list_invoices(*, start=0) -> Dict`
`POST crm.invoice.list.json`.

### `async list_activities(*, start=0, filter=None) -> Dict`
`POST crm.activity.list.json`.

### `async create_activity(fields) -> Dict`
`POST crm.activity.add.json`.

children.title: Tasks
children.content:
### `async list_tasks(*, start=0, filter=None, select=None, order=None) -> Dict`
`POST tasks.task.list.json` — result rows nested under `result.tasks`. Keys are camelCase (`id`, `title`, `responsibleId`).

### `async get_task(task_id) -> Dict`
`POST tasks.task.get.json` — body: `{taskId: task_id}`.

### `async create_task(title, responsible_id, description=None, fields=None) -> Dict`
`POST tasks.task.add.json` — body: `{fields: {TITLE, RESPONSIBLE_ID, DESCRIPTION?, ...}}`.

### `async update_task(task_id, fields) -> Dict`
`POST tasks.task.update.json`.

children.title: Disk · Im · Lists · Calendar
children.content:
### `async list_disk_children(folder_id) -> Dict`
`POST disk.folder.getchildren.json`.

### `async send_im_message(*, dialog_id, message, system=False) -> Dict`
`POST im.message.add.json` — body: `{DIALOG_ID, MESSAGE, SYSTEM?}`.

### `async list_lists_elements(iblock_id, *, filter=None) -> Dict`
`POST lists.element.get.json`.

### `async list_calendar_events(*, owner_id, type='user', from_date=None, to_date=None) -> Dict`
`POST calendar.event.get.json`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
Bitrix24Error                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── Bitrix24AuthError                  # 401 / 403 / expired_token / NO_AUTH_FOUND
├── Bitrix24BadRequestError            # 400
├── Bitrix24NotFoundError              # 404 / ERROR_NOT_FOUND
├── Bitrix24ConflictError              # 409
├── Bitrix24RateLimitError             # 429 / QUERY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED — retry_after_s
└── Bitrix24ServerError                # 5xx / transport
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
Bitrix24NetworkError    = Bitrix24ServerError    # legacy name
Bitrix24NotFound        = Bitrix24NotFoundError
Bitrix24APIError        = Bitrix24Error
Bitrix24ConnectorError  = Bitrix24Error
```

### Bitrix24's embedded-error envelope

Bitrix24 returns app-level errors as **HTTP 200** with a JSON body:

```json
{ "error": "QUERY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED", "error_description": "too many requests" }
```

The HTTP client maps:

| `error` token | Raises |
|---|---|
| `expired_token`, `invalid_token`, `NO_AUTH_FOUND`, `ACCESS_DENIED`, `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` | `Bitrix24AuthError` |
| `QUERY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED`, `OPERATION_TIME_LIMIT` | `Bitrix24RateLimitError` (retried) |
| `ERROR_NOT_FOUND`, `NOT_FOUND` | `Bitrix24NotFound` |
| any other | `Bitrix24Error` |

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Trigger | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 (HTTP) or `QUERY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` (embedded) | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `Bitrix24NetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `webhook_url` blank or malformed (missing `/rest/` segment) | Re-paste the full URL from Bitrix24 → Developer resources → Inbound webhook. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — webhook URL has been revoked or lacks scope | In Bitrix24 → Developer resources → Inbound webhook → Edit, tick the permissions you need (CRM/Tasks/User/etc.) and save. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — webhook URL deleted, or `access_token` expired (Mode B) | Re-issue the webhook URL OR refresh the OAuth token. |
| `Bitrix24RateLimitError: QUERY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | Hitting the per-second operating quota | The client retries 3× with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or chunk your calls. The free tier is 2 req/s. |
| 404 on `crm.*.get` | Wrong ID or the record was deleted | Verify the ID exists by calling `crm.*.list` first. |
| `tasks.task.list` returns empty `result.tasks` | `tasks.task.list` requires `select`/`filter` in some plans | Pass `select=['ID','TITLE','STATUS','RESPONSIBLE_ID']` explicitly. |
| `Bitrix24Error: NO_AUTH_FOUND` despite a valid URL | URL got truncated when pasted into a form field | Ensure the trailing slash is present and no characters were dropped. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'Bitrix24NetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `Bitrix24NetworkError = Bitrix24ServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
